*** 2 FATAL *** AN EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN FOUR MILES SOUTH OF LINDEN AND TRAVELED EAST-NORTHEAST FOR 13.9 MILES. A BRICK HOME ON HURRICANE CREEK RD RECEIVED DAMAGE CONCURRENT WITH WINDS AROUND 115 MPH. AT THIS POINT THE TORNADO HAD A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 500 YARDS. A HOUSE ON HIGHWAY 412 WAS SWEPT OF ITS FOUNDATION CAUSING TWO FATALITIES. THE TORNADO LIFTED ROUGHLY 5 MILES AFTER ENTERING HICKMAN COUNTY.

*** 7 INJ *** AN EF-3 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF THE HARDIN COUNTY/ WAYNE COUNTY LINE ABOUT 2.5 MILES SOUTHWEST OF THE TOWN OF LUTTS. THE TORNADO REACHED ITS PEAK STRENGTH ABOUT ONE HALF MILE WEST-SOUTHWEST OF LUTTS WHERE THE TORNADO REACHED A WIDTH OF 800 YARDS AND EF-3 STRENGTH. THE TORNADO WEAKENED AS IT CROSSED INTO MAURY COUNTY AND LIFTED 2 MILES SOUTHWEST OF MOUNT PLEASANT. THE TORNADO HAD ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 157 MPH AND A TOTAL PATH LENGTH WAS 50.6 MILES.

THIS TORNADO IS THE CONTINUATION OF THE EF-2 TORNADO WHICH TOUCHED DOWN IN LAUDERDALE COUNTY ALABAMA NEAR WATERLOO. THE TORNADO DAMAGE IN TENNESSEE WAS RATED AN EF-1 WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 110 MPH AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 400 YARDS. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 3.6 MILES IN TENNESSEE. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR BEVIS RIDGE ROAD IN WAYNE COUNTY.

AN EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON HELTON CREEK JUST SOUTHWEST OF DRY BRANCH ROAD IN DEKALB COUNTY. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO THE EAST-NORTHEAST AND REACHED A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 250 YARDS AS IT CROSSED HIGHWAY 264 AND SMITH FORK CREEK.
